market_context: Markets are under significant pressure with futures at yearly lows due to escalating Iran-Israel tensions driving oil prices higher and stoking stagflation fears. Private credit stress is weighing on financials as Morgan Stanley and JPMorgan restrict fund withdrawals and lending.

predictions.reasoning: Futures are down nearly 1% pre-market with energy-driven stagflation concerns dominating. Geopolitical tensions and broad risk-off sentiment should pressure the index lower.
Despite broad market weakness, tech has shown resilience with NVDA partnership news and AI infrastructure spending. QQQ may outperform on sector rotation into defensive growth names.
Earnings after market close with options implying 7.68% move. Recent 11% recovery and cheap 16x P/E valuation suggest positive surprise potential on AI integration progress.
Bank is actively marking down private credit loans and restricting sector exposure, signaling credit stress. Already down 2%+ pre-market with sector under scrutiny.
Morgan Stanley capped withdrawals from private credit fund with 11% redemption requests exceeding quarterly limits. Clear sign of stress in key revenue segment with contagion risk.
